newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Today's -7.2% drop (to $7.06) has no discrete catalyst and looks flow-driven: small-cap physical-AI de-risking plus lingering dilution/burn concerns (Q1 net loss ~$49M; Zacks notes 2026/2027 loss-per-share estimates widened to -$2.58/-$2.20 over the past week). Last real news was June 2 (pre-window): NoScrubs laundry-delivery vertical launch in LA, first commercial non-food vertical on the existing fleet.

sources.label: StockInvest SERV daily price/forecast page (Jun 8-9 prices, trend signals)
GlobeNewswire: Serve launches autonomous laundry vertical with NoScrubs (Jun 2, 2026)
Yahoo Finance/Zacks: Serve Robotics slips YTD, widening loss estimates
MarketBeat SERV news feed (checked for a June 9 catalyst; none found)

verdicts.rankRationale: Already #2 on the deployed physical-AI lens; today's -7% is flow-driven with the fleet/commercialization thesis intact, which modestly improves asymmetry, but widening loss estimates and near-certain dilution at a $0.6B cap argue against pushing it to #1 without new evidence.
verdicts.bull: Deployed, revenue-generating sidewalk fleet (Q1 revenue +578% YoY, 3x sequential) now monetizing a second vertical (NoScrubs laundry) on the same robots and autonomy stack; -40% YTD with thesis intact is improving asymmetry.
verdicts.neutral: No company-specific news since June 6; today's drop is sector/flow noise. Rank #2 already reflects the under-owned deployed-fleet case, so the setup is unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Q1 net loss ~$49M against a $0.6B cap means heavy dilution ahead; 2026/2027 loss estimates are widening, and new verticals (laundry, healthcare via Diligent) could be costly distraction rather than leverage.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of revenue-per-robot inflection or new vertical/metro contracts (next checkpoint: Aug 6 Q2 earnings).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Dilutive capital raise into a falling tape given ~$78M+ annualized cash burn.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the scaling watchlist the name is pure 'autonomous delivery optionality'; a -7% flow-driven dip slightly cheapens that optionality but yesterday's adjusted score already sat 3 places below the displayed rank, and widening loss estimates offset the better entry, so rank 18 stands.
verdicts.bull: Optionality is broadening, not narrowing: same fleet now serves food plus laundry with dry cleaning, pharmacy, and grocery as adjacent verticals, and the stock is 40% cheaper YTD with revenue compounding.
verdicts.neutral: Nothing material since June 6; the move is beta, not thesis. Optionality bucket position around rank 18 remains fair versus the adjusted score.
verdicts.bear: Optionality funded by equity: losses widening to an estimated -$2.58/share in 2026 means the option premium is paid via dilution, and a sub-$1B cap robotics name can derate further in a risk-off tape.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: NoScrubs pilot traction or another non-food vertical converting to a commercial contract.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Funding overhang and continued estimate cuts compressing the optionality value before any vertical scales.
